nodes.text: Here is the myth of you: you were born in a world that had already decided what you were. You have been arguing with that decision ever since.
The argument is the story. Not the conclusion — the argument.
He's either the wisest person you've ever met or the kind of crazy that sounds like wisdom.
The distinction, you're realizing, may not matter as much as you thought.

opts.text: [LOGIC] The coffee appeared without anyone taking an order. The booth was empty when you walked in.

nodes.text: Something old. Something that remembers when the stories were new.
Something that has been watching your kind make the same mistakes for so long that the mistakes have started to look like a tradition.
I find it, against all reason, charming.
Because you are at a crossroads. Not the metaphorical kind — though those too. The actual kind.
You came here looking for a direction. I'm one of the things that lives at crossroads.
The coffee. The empty booth. The way he knew your face.
You add it up slowly, the way you do when you don't want the answer to be true.
There it is. Yes. You're putting it together.
Most people look right at the seams and see upholstery. You're different.
